title: Nothing notices when the live daemon runs stale code - record git state at daemon startup + passive staleness check
source: Claude Fable improvement pass 2026-07-07 - the live daemon ran pre-T-270 code for weeks (l3_cache frozen at exactly 1000 rows, gmail_send still really sending, watcher alerts dead) and no mechanism flagged it
current_state: pi_daemon.py tracks _start_time/uptime (lines ~125, ~277) but records nothing about WHICH code it is running. The repo moved ~30 tickets ahead of the running process and every fixed bug stayed live in production: L3 truncation, real email sends, unauthorized button taps. Discovery was pure luck (a live SQLite query during an unrelated diagnosis). There is no check - passive or otherwise - comparing the running daemon's code vintage to the repo's current state.
target_state: At startup, pi_daemon writes data/daemon_info.json: {git_rev (git rev-parse HEAD, best-effort), dirty_file_count (git status --porcelain | wc -l), started_at}. A new passive skill scripts/passive/daemon_staleness_checker.py (clone the structure of an existing small passive like turns_log_rotate/silent_failure_watcher) reads it and reports: WARN if the daemon's git_rev != current repo HEAD, or if any tracked .py file's mtime is newer than started_at; FAIL if the drift exceeds 7 days. Wired into the /digest set. Output names the exact remedy: 'restart the daemon'. Test: unit test the comparison logic with synthetic daemon_info + tmp files (no real git needed - inject the rev strings).
migration_plan: 1. Test first (testing/test_daemon_staleness_checker.py): feed the checker a daemon_info dict + a tmp tree where one .py mtime is newer than started_at -> WARN with the file named; same rev + no newer files -> PASS; missing daemon_info.json -> WARN 'daemon not running or predates T-284'.

2. In pi_daemon.py startup (next to the existing _start_time assignment ~line 277), write data/daemon_info.json via subprocess git rev-parse HEAD (best-effort try/except with track_silent - git may be absent).
3. New scripts/passive/daemon_staleness_checker.py using scripts/passive/common.py helpers (Status, write_report, status_to_exit_code) - keep it under ~120 lines like its siblings.
4. Register it in the /digest skill list (wherever the 13 passive skills are enumerated).
5. python scripts/verify.py must print PASS (never pipe, T-214); SOLUTIONS.jsonl; close; refresh_pi.py.
risk_notes: The daemon-side write is best-effort and must never block startup (track_silent on failure). Do not auto-restart the daemon from the checker - passive skills are read-only by contract; the check TELLS Ash to restart. mtime comparison is heuristic (a touched file with no change still warns) - acceptable, the failure mode is a spurious restart suggestion, not silence.
